# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function preview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');
        if (!filter_var($url, FILTER_VALIDATE_URL)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 400);
        }

        $ch = curl_init();

        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_URL, $url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 10);

        $response = curl_exec($ch);

        if(curl_errno($ch)) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 500);
        }

        curl_close($ch);

        $dom = new \DOMDocument();
        @$dom->loadHTML($response);

        $title = $dom->getElementsByTagName('title')->item(0);
        $titleText = $title ? $title->textContent : 'No title found';

        $metaTags = $dom->getElementsByTagName('meta');
        $description = '';

        foreach ($metaTags as $meta) {
            if ($meta->getAttribute('name') == 'description') {
                $description = $meta->getAttribute('content');
                break;
            }
        }

        return response()->json([
            'title' => $titleText,
            'description' => $description
        ]);
    }
}
